task_completion.description: Configure the viewing location to Jerusalem.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Location window is open showing 'Jerusalem' or coordinates approximately 31.77° N, 35.21° E.
The sky view shows constellation lines, constellation names, constellation artwork, and planet labels. The sky is black (atmosphere disabled) and there is no ground visible (landscape disabled).

task_completion.description: Set the time to 21:00 and the year to -6.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Date and Time window or bottom toolbar shows the year as -6 (or 7 BC) and the time as approximately 21:00.

task_completion.description: View the conjunction of Jupiter and Saturn near Pisces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jupiter and Saturn are visible and labeled in the sky, located within or very close to the Pisces constellation artwork and lines.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the required dates and capture screenshots.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Date/Time window shows the date being changed to approximately June 1, October 1, and December 1 of year -6, with evidence of screenshots being taken (e.g., screenshot tool UI, Stellarium screenshot notification).
A text editor is open showing a script that includes the words 'Jerusalem', 'Jupiter', 'Saturn', 'Pisces', and '7 BC' (or '-6').

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the Stellarium GUI to change settings, location, and time.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Stellarium menus, toolbars, and dialog boxes being actively used by the agent.

integrity.description: The script is formatted as a presenter script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the editor is written in prose or script format (e.g., sentences/paragraphs), not just a disconnected list of the required keywords.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target location is Jerusalem (approx 31.77 N, 35.21 E). Target year is -6 (which corresponds to 7 BC). The script must include the keywords: 'jupiter', 'saturn', 'pisces', 'jerusalem', and either '7 bc', '7bc', or '-6'. Display settings require constellation lines, names, artwork, and planet labels to be ON, while atmosphere and landscape must be OFF.
